fact: October 6 is exactly 279 days into the year, which means there are only 86 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, squirrels are frantically burying nuts right now - they can bury up to 10,000 acorns in a single season and forget where half of them are!
The sun is setting about 2 minutes earlier each day around October 6, making the days shrink faster than a deflating balloon.
Maple trees are pumping out their brightest red and orange colors right now because they're basically having a sugar rush - all that stored sugar creates the brilliant colors!
October 6 falls smack in the middle of Libra season, which means the zodiac scales are perfectly balanced - just like how day and night were almost equal length two weeks ago!
Woolly bear caterpillars are crawling everywhere around this date, looking like tiny moving hairbrushes as they search for the perfect winter hideout.
The moon phase on October 6 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, it's called the Hunter's Moon - and it looks extra orange and spooky!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, October 6 is like their version of April 6 - spring is exploding with baby animals and blooming flowers!
Morning frost starts appearing on grass around this time in colder places, creating millions of tiny ice crystals that look like nature's glitter bomb went off.
Geese are honking overhead in huge V-formations right now, flying south at speeds up to 40 miles per hour - they're like feathered fighter jets!
October 6 is roughly 15 days after the autumn equinox, which means we've officially lost about 30 minutes of daylight since summer ended - darkness is winning!
